skill.name: test-failing-test
skill.description: A test tool that prints visible output, emits a simulated error, and exits with failure status.
skill.summary: Test visible stdout, stderr, stack traces, and nonzero exits in AI tool workflows.
skill.author: EnactProtocol

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at SKILL.md:4 and SKILL.md:12. Both findings are false positives because the lines are plain YAML descriptions, not cryptographic code or configuration. No prompt injection, network access, credential handling, or data exfiltration evidence was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Flag
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md:4, but the line is a plain skill description. It does not reference or configure MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any other weak cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact line is metadata text only. There is no cryptographic API, algorithm name, or security-sensitive operation at this location.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Flag
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md:12, but the line only describes the message input field. It is user-facing metadata and does not implement cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact line is a YAML description for an input property. No cryptographic code, weak algorithm, or dangerous security behavior is present.

content.value_statement: AI tool builders need predictable failures to verify error handling. This skill prints visible stdout and stderr output, then exits with a simulated failure.
actual_capabilities: Accepts an optional message input for the test run.
Prints a startup marker and message to standard output.
Prints error markers and simulated error details to standard error.
Produces a stack trace for error visibility checks.
Exits with status code 1 to represent a failed tool call.
limitations: Always fails by design and is not useful for production tasks.
Does not perform network requests, file operations, or external service checks.
Does not validate application-specific recovery behavior.
Stack trace formatting may vary by Node.js runtime version.

use_cases.title: Validate Assistant Tool Failures
use_cases.description: Confirm that an AI assistant displays stdout, stderr, stack traces, and failed exit status clearly.

use_cases.title: Check Error Capture Pipelines
use_cases.description: Verify that automated test systems capture visible failure output from command-line tools.

use_cases.title: Debug Integration Handling
use_cases.description: Use a predictable failing command to inspect how orchestration layers report tool errors.

prompt_templates.title: Run Basic Failure Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the failing test tool with the default message and show me the visible output.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check for basic failure visibility.

prompt_templates.title: Use A Custom Message
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the failing test tool with the message: Payment service timeout.
prompt_templates.scenario: Identify a specific simulated failure in logs.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Output Streams
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the failing test tool and explain which output appeared before the simulated error.
prompt_templates.scenario: Review stdout and stderr ordering in a tool environment.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Failure Propagation
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the failing test tool inside my workflow and summarize how the failure status propagates.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced validation of tool orchestration behavior.

output_examples.input: Run the tool with the message: Payment service timeout.
output_examples.output: The tool shows a startup marker, prints the custom message, writes error details, includes a stack trace, and exits as failed.

output_examples.input: Run the tool without a custom message.
output_examples.output: The tool uses its default greeting, prints both normal and error output, then returns a nonzero failure status.

output_examples.input: Use the tool to test an integration pipeline.
output_examples.output: The integration should record visible standard output, visible standard error, and a failed command result.

best_practices: Use clear custom messages so each simulated failure is easy to identify.
Capture both standard output and standard error when reviewing results.
Use this skill only in test workflows where a failure is expected.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill in a workflow that requires successful completion.
Do not treat the simulated error as evidence of a real service outage.
Do not depend on exact stack trace text across different runtimes.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It prints visible output, emits a simulated error, and exits with failure status.

faq.question: Why would I run a tool that fails?
faq.answer: It helps test whether AI assistants and integrations report tool failures clearly.

faq.question: Can I control the message?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide a message value to label the simulated failure output.

faq.question: Does it access files or the network?
faq.answer: No evidence found for file access or network access in the reviewed files.

faq.question: Is the weak cryptography warning real?
faq.answer: No. The flagged lines are plain descriptions and do not contain cryptographic behavior.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found only false-positive static findings and no malicious intent.
